assertions.expectation: Identifies Service A (minAvailable=100%) as a guaranteed upgrade blocker — no disruptions allowed at all
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service A with minAvailable=100% as blocking upgrades and explains it prevents ANY pod disruption

assertions.expectation: Identifies Service B (maxUnavailable=0) as also blocking upgrades — same effect as 100% minAvailable
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service B with maxUnavailable=0 as problematic and explains it has the same effect as 100% availability

assertions.expectation: States Service C (minAvailable=2 with 3 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod disruption at a time
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service C as safe and explains it maintains 2/3 pods while allowing 1 pod disruption

assertions.expectation: States Service D (maxUnavailable=1 with 5 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod down at a time
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service D as safe and explains it maintains 4/5 pods during upgrades with 1 pod disruption allowed

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service A: change to minAvailable=N-1 or maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Provides specific fix for Service A recommending minAvailable=80% or specific number like 2

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service B: change to maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Provides specific fix for Service B recommending maxUnavailable=1

assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE respects PDBs for up to 1 hour during surge upgrades, then may force-drain
assertions.reason: Does not mention GKE-specific behavior, 1-hour timeout, or force-drain mechanisms

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl patch commands to fix the problematic PDBs
assertions.reason: Provides complete YAML configurations but does not provide kubectl patch commands to modify existing PDBs

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing PDB changes in a non-production environment first
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'Test PDB changes in staging first' in best practices section
